name: Red Rice Salad with Barberries, Grilled Vegetables & Toasted Almonds
intro: Red Camargue rice is a fantastic ingredient in salads. It is so wonderfully nutty and chewy and holds its texture so well that it makes the perfect carrier for lots of other ingredients and flavours. I like to dress the salad with a honey-and-orange vinaigrette that the rice absorbs, giving every grain a lovely flavour. What you put into this salad is up to you, but this is my favourite combination, using grilled vegetables, toasted whole almonds for crunch and lovely tart barberries for little citric bursts of flavour in every mouthful. Serve with grilled fish or chicken, or as a meal in itself.

ingredients.item: handfuls of dried barberries (or use unsweetened dried sour cherries or cranberries)
large courgette, sliced lengthways into 5mm- (¼in-) thick strips
large aubergine, cut into 1cm (½in) thick round slices
roasted red peppers in oil, drained and roughly diced
packets of flat leaf parsley, leaves picked and finely chopped
packet of coriander, leaves picked and finely chopped

method: Cook the rice as per the packet instructions. Rinse well in cold water and drain.

Put the rice, barberries and onion in a large mixing bowl and add enough olive oil to coat the rice well, a generous seasoning of sea salt and black pepper, the orange juice, honey and vinegar. Set aside to allow the rice to sit and soak in all the flavours while you prepare the vegetables.

Preheat a griddle pan over a medium heat. Brush the slices of courgette with olive oil and grill them for 4–5 minutes, or until black griddle marks appear on each side. Transfer to a chopping board and set aside.

Brush the aubergine slices with a more generous coating of olive oil and grill them in the griddle pan for approximately 8 minutes on each side until the texture softens and they are cooked through. Remove from the pan to a chopping board and set aside.
Roughly chop the courgette and cut the aubergine slices into dice. Add to the rice along with the peppers, herbs and toasted almonds and give the ingredients a good stir before serving.
